name: Ashgabat
summary: Ashgabat is generally safe for tourists with very low violent crime rates and limited petty crime due to strict government controls. The main safety concerns include rigid travel restrictions, earthquake risks, and limited healthcare facilities.
quickVerdict: Ashgabat offers a unique travel experience with excellent personal safety and low crime rates in a highly controlled environment. While tourists rarely encounter violent crime or serious safety issues, the city requires careful planning due to visa restrictions, mandatory guides, and limited medical services. The seismic risk and extreme summer heat are natural considerations, but overall the city maintains a secure atmosphere for visitors who follow local rules.

neighborhoods.name: Central Ashgabat
neighborhoods.description: The heart of the capital featuring white marble buildings and government offices. Well-patrolled and safest area for tourists with good infrastructure.

neighborhoods.name: Kopetdag District
neighborhoods.description: Cultural district known for museums and landmarks. Safe and welcoming area with good access to historical sites and cultural attractions.

neighborhoods.name: Chandybil District
neighborhoods.description: Scenic district offering beautiful landscapes and outdoor activities. Quieter residential area perfect for those seeking a more relaxed atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Bezmeinski District
neighborhoods.description: Mixed residential and commercial area with moderate foot traffic. Generally safe but requires normal urban precautions especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Fourth and Fifth Districts
neighborhoods.description: Areas known to have higher but still moderate crime rates. Exercise additional caution particularly after dark and avoid isolated areas.

neighborhoods.name: Outskirts and Rural Areas
neighborhoods.description: Peripheral areas with limited security and infrastructure. Avoid at night and travel only with proper guides and authorization.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unregulated taxi drivers may charge excessive fares claiming broken meters or inflating prices for foreigners.
scams.howToAvoid: Use the government-regulated taxi company at +993 12 32-97-75 or arrange transport through your hotel. Always agree on price beforehand.

scams.name: Fake Tour Guides
scams.description: Unofficial guides may approach tourists offering tours at inflated prices or leading to shops where they receive commissions.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use officially licensed guides arranged through your hotel or tour operator. Verify credentials through official channels.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Manipulation
scams.description: Street money changers may offer poor exchange rates or provide counterfeit bills to unsuspecting tourists.
scams.howToAvoid: Exchange money only through official banks and authorized exchange points. Avoid street exchanges entirely.

scams.name: Souvenir Overpricing
scams.description: Tourist markets may significantly inflate prices for carpets, handicrafts, and traditional items targeting foreign visitors.
scams.howToAvoid: Research fair prices beforehand and negotiate. Consider purchasing from government stores or through your guide for authentic items.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Ashgabat requires cultural sensitivity and conservative dress. While violent crime is rare, women face higher scrutiny due to conservative social norms. Modest clothing and reserved behavior are strongly advised to avoid unwanted attention.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees, avoid tight or revealing clothing
Avoid public displays of affection or close friendships with local men
Stay in established hotels and avoid isolated areas especially after dark
Carry contact information for your embassy and maintain regular communication with home

nightSafety.overview: Ashgabat is relatively safe at night with low violent crime rates, but the city quiets down significantly after dark. Most restaurants and entertainment venues close early, and street lighting may be limited in some areas.
tips: Use official taxis rather than walking at night
Stay in well-lit central areas and avoid deserted neighborhoods
Plan evening activities early as most venues close by 10 PM
Travel in groups when possible and inform others of your plans

transport.metro: Ashgabat does not have a metro system. Public transport is limited and not tourist-friendly.
transport.rideshare: International rideshare apps are not available. Transportation options are limited to official taxis and arranged tours.
transport.taxis: One government-regulated taxi company operates in the city charging a flat fee of about $2.80 within city limits. Most other taxis are unregulated private operators without proper licensing or safety equipment.
transport.tips: Use the official taxi service at +993 12 32-97-75 or arrange transport through your hotel. Avoid unregulated taxis especially after dark.

customs: Respect for elders is paramount - always greet seniors first and show deference
Hospitality is sacred - guests are welcomed with 'Khosh geldiniz' and treated with great respect
Remove shoes when entering homes and avoid stepping on bread or carpets
Traditional greetings involve handshakes among men, but men and women typically greet verbally
Photography of government buildings, military installations, and sensitive areas is strictly prohibited

health.overview: Healthcare in Turkmenistan significantly lags behind Western standards with limited facilities and supplies. Medical evacuation insurance is strongly recommended as serious conditions require treatment abroad.
health.water: Tap water is not safe for drinking. Use bottled water for drinking and brushing teeth. Exercise caution with ice and raw vegetables.
health.vaccinations: Standard travel vaccinations recommended. Consult CDC guidelines for current requirements including hepatitis A/B and typhoid.
health.altitude: Ashgabat sits at moderate elevation. Main health concerns are extreme summer heat and poor air quality from dust storms.

emergency.usEmbassy: +993 12 94-00-45 (local) / US emergency line +1-202-501-4444. Located at 9 1984 Street, Ashgabat 744000.

faq.a: Yes, Ashgabat is generally very safe for tourists with low crime rates due to strict government controls. However, visitors must follow rigid rules and travel restrictions including mandatory guides outside the city.
Ashgabat is relatively safe at night with low violent crime, but most venues close early and street lighting is limited. Use official taxis and stay in central well-lit areas after dark.

faq.q: Is Ashgabat safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travelers can visit safely but must dress conservatively and be aware of cultural sensitivities. Avoid public displays of affection and maintain reserved behavior to respect local norms.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Ashgabat?
faq.a: Avoid the fourth, fifth, and sixth districts at night, as well as rural outskirts and border areas. Stay in central districts and tourist zones, always with proper guides when outside the city center.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Ashgabat?
faq.a: Public transport options are very limited. Use the government-regulated taxi service or arrange transport through your hotel rather than unregulated private taxis which may lack safety equipment.
